title: JIT GC safepoints and heap allocation
purpose: JIT-compiled code that allocates on the heap (put_tuple, put_list, put_map) must coordinate with the garbage collector. GC safepoints are locations in compiled code where GC can safely interrupt execution, and the stack/register map is known.
task: Implement GC safepoint emission in compiled code and heap allocation primitives callable from JIT code.

requirements.title: GC safepoint emission
requirements.spec: Emit safepoint metadata at allocation sites in compiled code.
acceptance: Each heap allocation site in compiled code has a safepoint descriptor
Safepoint records which registers hold live Term values (the root set)
GC can interrupt at safepoints and relocate heap objects
Test: compiled function with allocation survives GC

requirements.title: Heap allocation from compiled code
requirements.spec: Compiled code calls into runtime helpers for heap allocation.
acceptance: Helpers trigger GC if heap is full, then retry allocation
Compiled code calls these via Cranelift's call instruction
Test: compiled put_tuple allocates correctly on the heap

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement on-stack replacement (OSR) — compiled code runs from function entry
SHALL NOT implement escape analysis — all boxed terms go to the heap
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
